requested_deliverable: Deep-research breakdown of practical replacements for GitHub Copilot-style live autocomplete in VS Code and Obsidian, prioritizing existing GPT or Claude subscriptions where possible.

notes: This report synthesizes completed worker handoffs T000, T005, T010, T020, T030, T040, and T045.
No extension or plugin was installed or runtime-tested during synthesis.
Do not treat this report as final factual verification; queued follow-up tasks T055 and T060 should check schema, traceability, and current facts.

claim_labeling.recommendation: Decision guidance derived from source-backed facts and stated assumptions.
claim_labeling.inference: Reasoned conclusion from cited facts; not directly stated by a source.
claim_labeling.preference: A value judgment such as privacy-first or low-setup preference.
claim_labeling.speculation: Forward-looking or unverified possibility; not used as a primary basis.

bottom_line.text: There is no robust, official, current all-in-one way to use an existing ChatGPT or Claude UI subscription for Copilot-style live Tab autocomplete in both VS Code and Obsidian. Use official subscription tools such as OpenAI Codex or Claude Code for agent/chat/edit workflows, but use local models, product-specific autocomplete plans, or separately billed APIs for true live autocomplete.

citations.label: Claude subscription vs API/Console Help Center article

key_findings.text: OpenAI Codex can be used through ChatGPT sign-in for covered plans or through an API-key path, but OpenAI Codex IDE docs describe sidebar/chat/edit/cloud-agent workflows rather than inline Tab autocomplete.

Claude paid plans and Claude API/Console are separate products; Claude Code can use eligible Claude subscriptions, but checked Claude Code VS Code docs describe agentic plans/diffs/chat, not live inline Tab autocomplete.

citations.label: Claude subscription vs API/Console Help Center article
Use Claude Code with Pro or Max plan
Use Claude Code with Team or Enterprise plan

key_findings.text: VS Code has several credible true inline autocomplete alternatives: Continue, Kilo Code, Windsurf/Codeium, Supermaven, Tabby, and llama.vscode. They generally use local models, vendor accounts/plans, or API keys rather than ChatGPT/Claude UI subscriptions for autocomplete.

Obsidian has true live ghost-text/Tab autocomplete community plugins, especially AI Autocomplete, Inscribe, Copilot auto completion, Companion, and Markpilot, but the practical paths found are API-key/OpenAI-compatible/local endpoints, not existing ChatGPT or Claude subscriptions.
For code autocomplete, FIM-capable models/endpoints are better-shaped than generic chat prompting because editor completion naturally has prefix and suffix around the cursor.

For both editors with one coherent backend, build around a shared local or API-backed /autocomplete broker, use an existing VS Code client where possible, and use either an existing Obsidian inline plugin or a small CodeMirror plugin for Obsidian-specific ghost text.

primary_path.text: If the goal is practical replacement rather than subscription reuse purity: use Continue or Kilo/Windsurf/Supermaven in VS Code, and use Inscribe or AI Autocomplete in Obsidian. Prefer local Ollama/llama.cpp/Qwen2.5-Coder for no API spend/privacy, or Mistral Codestral/OpenAI-compatible APIs if separate billing is acceptable.

do_not_do_by_default.text: Do not build the main workflow on browser-cookie/session scraping or unofficial ChatGPT/Claude session proxies. They are unsupported, brittle, security-sensitive, and poor fits for low-latency autocomplete.

subscription_reuse_exception_to_watch.text: A non-official VS Code extension named Codex Autocomplete claims Codex OAuth-based inline Tab autocomplete using existing Codex plan limits/credits, but it is macOS-only, tiny, non-official, and uses a chatgpt.com backend endpoint; treat it as an experimental candidate requiring policy/security/runtime review, not the default recommendation.

decision_paths.recommendation: Use local models or free/product-specific autocomplete services; do not expect ChatGPT/Claude UI subscriptions to power arbitrary autocomplete plugins.
path: VS Code: start with Continue + Ollama qwen2.5-coder or llama.vscode; if self-hosting for a team, evaluate Tabby.

Obsidian: start with Inscribe configured to Ollama/local OpenAI-compatible endpoint; if that is insufficient, use Copilot auto completion or build a small CodeMirror plugin against the same local broker.
If local hardware is weak, Windsurf or Kilo/Supermaven can provide VS Code autocomplete without API keys, but this does not solve Obsidian with the same subscription.
tradeoffs: Requires local setup and possibly weaker latency/quality on weak hardware
No GPT/Claude subscription reuse except near-scope agent workflows

decision_paths.recommendation: Use purpose-built autocomplete/FIM APIs where possible rather than generic chat models; accept that OpenAI/Anthropic API use is separate from ChatGPT/Claude subscriptions.
path: VS Code: Continue with Mistral Codestral FIM or another FIM-capable provider; Kilo autocomplete through Kilo Gateway/BYOK; Tabby/Continue through OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
Obsidian: AI Autocomplete or Inscribe with OpenAI-compatible endpoints; use Copilot auto completion if its older but mature FIM-oriented controls matter more than recent maintenance.
For Claude/OpenAI APIs, budget for frequent keystroke-adjacent calls and keep max_tokens/context small.
tradeoffs: Better hosted quality and less local ops
Separate API billing and remote privacy exposure
Chat models may underperform FIM-specific autocomplete models

citations.label: Claude subscription vs API/Console Help Center article

decision_paths.recommendation: Use a local FIM-capable stack and keep editor plugins thin.
path: Run llama.cpp /infill or Ollama locally with Qwen2.5-Coder, StarCoder2, or another FIM-capable code model.
VS Code: Continue, llama.vscode, or Tabby depending on desired setup weight.
Obsidian: Inscribe with Ollama/local endpoint first; if UX is not good enough, build a CodeMirror ghost-text plugin that calls the local /autocomplete broker.
tradeoffs: Strongest privacy and no per-token bill
Prose note completions may need different models/prompts than code

decision_paths.assumption: Highest autocomplete quality, subscription reuse not required
decision_paths.recommendation: For VS Code-like code autocomplete quality, prefer mature commercial autocomplete or a VS Code fork; for Obsidian, expect a less mature plugin ecosystem or a custom plugin.
path: If switching editors is acceptable: evaluate Cursor Tab as near-scope because it is a VS Code fork, not a stock VS Code extension.
Inside stock VS Code: evaluate Windsurf, Supermaven, Kilo Code, and Continue with a strong FIM provider.
In Obsidian: compare AI Autocomplete, Inscribe, and Copilot auto completion with the same provider; runtime-test latency and Tab conflicts before committing.
tradeoffs: Best UX may require non-GPT/Claude plans or switching editor
Obsidian quality is less certain because top inline plugins are smaller/younger or older

subscription_constraints.summary: ChatGPT plans can include Codex usage and Codex supports ChatGPT sign-in, but arbitrary API use and third-party autocomplete plugins should not be treated as covered by the ChatGPT UI subscription. API-key use is a separate platform path with token pricing.
Claude paid subscriptions do not include Claude API/Console access. Claude Code is available through eligible subscriptions, but checked VS Code docs do not show live inline Tab autocomplete.
subscription_constraints.official_subscription_agent_surface: Claude Code terminal/VS Code agent workflows
subscription_constraints.future_near_scope: Claude Agent SDK monthly credit starts 2026-06-15 per source, but it is future-dated and not an already available autocomplete path.

citations.label: Claude subscription vs API/Console Help Center article
Use Claude Code with Pro or Max plan
Use Claude Code with Team or Enterprise plan
Claude Agent SDK monthly plan credit

option_matrix.name: Continue
option_matrix.short_rationale: Best configurable open-source VS Code path; docs are FIM-aware and warn chat/thinking models can be poor/slow for autocomplete.

option_matrix.name: Kilo Code
option_matrix.short_rationale: Current high-install open-source extension with documented inline ghost-text Tab autocomplete; do not conflate its ChatGPT OAuth agent provider with autocomplete billing.

citations.label: Kilo Code ChatGPT Plus/Pro provider docs

option_matrix.name: Windsurf VS Code Plugin / Codeium
option_matrix.short_rationale: Low-setup, free/commercial inline autocomplete; full Windsurf Tab/Supercomplete is editor-exclusive, not fully in VS Code plugin.

option_matrix.name: Supermaven
option_matrix.short_rationale: Strong commercial autocomplete baseline with vendor latency/context claims; plugin remains maintained after joining Cursor, but Cursor is core focus.

option_matrix.name: Tabby self-hosted coding assistant
option_matrix.short_rationale: Turnkey self-hosted/on-prem VS Code completion stack with FIM-oriented completion model role; does not itself solve Obsidian true inline UX.

option_matrix.name: llama.vscode
option_matrix.short_rationale: Lightweight local VS Code completion using llama.cpp and FIM-compatible models; quality/latency depend on hardware.

option_matrix.name: Codex Autocomplete
option_matrix.short_rationale: Closest found VS Code subscription-reuse candidate, but non-official, low-maturity, macOS-only, and not runtime/policy reviewed.

option_matrix.name: OpenAI Codex official IDE extension
option_matrix.short_rationale: Official subscription-backed coding assistant, but checked docs describe chat/edit/cloud tasks rather than live Tab autocomplete.

option_matrix.name: Claude Code official VS Code/CLI
option_matrix.short_rationale: Official Claude subscription coding tool, but checked VS Code docs describe plans/diffs/chat/terminal workflows rather than live Tab autocomplete.

citations.label: Use Claude Code with Pro or Max plan
Claude subscription vs API/Console Help Center article

option_matrix.name: AI Autocomplete
option_matrix.short_rationale: Closest low-friction Obsidian ghost-text/Tab plugin found, but very new and low adoption.

option_matrix.name: Inscribe
option_matrix.short_rationale: Strong ready-made Obsidian inline option with Ollama/local support and current maintenance.

option_matrix.name: Copilot auto completion
option_matrix.short_rationale: Mature purpose-built Obsidian Copilot-like autocomplete with FIM-oriented context, but weaker maintenance recency.

citations.label: Copilot auto completion Obsidian plugin listing

option_matrix.name: Companion
option_matrix.short_rationale: Higher adoption and mobile support, but older maintenance and Tab behavior may be word-level rather than whole suggestion.

option_matrix.name: Claudian
option_matrix.short_rationale: Major Obsidian Claude/Codex agent plugin with large signal, but it is sidebar/inline-edit/agent workflow, not live ghost-text autocomplete.

option_matrix.name: Shared local autocomplete broker
option_matrix.short_rationale: Most coherent cross-editor architecture if you are willing to operate/build a small backend and possibly an Obsidian plugin.

option_matrix.name: Shared API-key autocomplete broker
option_matrix.short_rationale: Cleanly separates editor UX from providers and lets both editors share rate limits, context shaping, and secret storage.

steps: Install and trial Windsurf, Kilo Code, or Supermaven in VS Code for immediate inline Tab autocomplete; also trial Continue if you want model/provider control.
Install Inscribe and AI Autocomplete in Obsidian in a disposable vault/profile and configure either Ollama/local or an OpenAI-compatible API endpoint.
Compare latency, suggestion quality, Tab conflicts, and privacy settings before replacing Copilot.
implementation_paths.best_when: You want a practical result soon and can accept separate products/accounts or local/API setup.
implementation_paths.not_good_when: You require one existing ChatGPT/Claude subscription to cover everything.

steps: Start a local llama.cpp /infill or Ollama endpoint with Qwen2.5-Coder or StarCoder2.
Use Continue or llama.vscode in VS Code against that local endpoint.
Use Inscribe in Obsidian against Ollama/local OpenAI-compatible endpoint; if the UX is insufficient, build a CodeMirror ghost-text plugin that calls a local /autocomplete broker.
Keep context windows small, use low temperature, cap completions, debounce, and cancel stale requests.
implementation_paths.best_when: Privacy/no API billing matters more than plug-and-play polish.
implementation_paths.not_good_when: You lack local CPU/GPU capacity or do not want local model ops.

steps: Define a local /autocomplete broker accepting editor, document_uri, language, prefix, suffix, selection, max_tokens, stop, and request_id.
Implement adapters for Mistral Codestral /v1/fim/completions, llama.cpp /infill, Ollama generate/chat, OpenRouter/LiteLLM, and optional OpenAI/Anthropic chat fallbacks.
VS Code can use Continue or a custom InlineCompletionItemProvider. Obsidian can use an existing plugin if it accepts your endpoint or a custom CodeMirror widget/keymap plugin.
Centralize secret storage, logging policy, rate limits, and remote/local indicators in the broker.
implementation_paths.best_when: You want one backend and are comfortable with separate API billing or mixed local/remote providers.

steps: Use OpenAI Codex or Claude Code in VS Code for agentic coding with your existing subscription where supported.
Use Obsidian Claudian, Claude Panel, Blackglass, or similar tools only for chat/agent/inline-edit/vault workflows, not live autocomplete.
Pair those agent tools with a separate true autocomplete path if Tab completion remains required.
implementation_paths.best_when: You mainly want agentic edits/chat and only secondarily care about live autocomplete.
implementation_paths.not_good_when: Your hard requirement is continuous ghost-text Tab completion.

detailed_options.name: Continue
detailed_options.summary: Open-source, configurable VS Code autocomplete/client stack; best when you want provider/local control and can tune FIM-capable models.

facts.text: Continue docs describe inline autocomplete and recommend FIM-oriented autocomplete models; provider docs distinguish model roles.

recommendation.text: Use as first VS Code choice for local/API experimentation.

limitations: No direct ChatGPT/Claude UI subscription autocomplete path.
Cloud providers incur separate API billing.
detailed_options.best_pairing_for_obsidian: Inscribe or a custom Obsidian plugin against the same Ollama/llama.cpp/API broker.

detailed_options.name: Kilo Code
detailed_options.summary: Current high-install VS Code extension with documented inline Tab autocomplete and also ChatGPT Plus/Pro OAuth for agent workflows.

facts.text: Kilo autocomplete docs route FIM autocomplete through Kilo Gateway/BYOK; separate docs describe ChatGPT Plus/Pro OAuth for core agent workflows.

citations.label: Kilo Code ChatGPT Plus/Pro provider docs

recommendation.text: Evaluate for VS Code, but do not assume ChatGPT subscription pays for autocomplete until verified.

limitations: Autocomplete billing/provider path needs separate review.
Large agent feature surface may require permission/privacy review.

detailed_options.name: Windsurf or Supermaven
detailed_options.summary: Low-setup commercial autocomplete alternatives inside VS Code.

facts.text: Windsurf docs document inline/multiline Tab autocomplete and FIM; Supermaven sources claim low-latency large-context completion.

recommendation.text: Use if you value plug-and-play VS Code quality more than GPT/Claude subscription reuse or open-source control.

detailed_options.name: Tabby or llama.vscode

facts.text: Tabby documents a FIM-oriented completion model role and real-time VS Code completions; llama.vscode documents local llama.cpp completions with Tab accept and FIM-compatible models.

recommendation.text: Use Tabby for heavier self-host/team setup; use llama.vscode for a lightweight local VS Code experiment.

limitations: Obsidian still requires separate plugin/client path.

detailed_options.name: Obsidian true-inline plugins
detailed_options.summary: AI Autocomplete, Inscribe, Copilot auto completion, Companion, and Markpilot are the main ready-made true inline/Tab Obsidian candidates.

facts.text: T030 found README/listing/source evidence of ghost text and Tab accept or equivalent in these plugins.

recommendation.text: Start with Inscribe for local/API flexibility or AI Autocomplete for low-friction OpenAI-compatible/Groq setup; keep Copilot auto completion as an older mature fallback.

limitations: No direct ChatGPT/Claude subscription reuse found.
Plugin maturity varies; runtime smoke test needed.
Remote APIs send note/cursor context repeatedly.

detailed_options.name: OpenAI Codex and Claude Code official tools
detailed_options.summary: Useful subscription-backed agent tools but not replacements for live Tab autocomplete.

facts.text: Official OpenAI and Anthropic sources document subscription access for Codex/Claude Code respectively, while checked feature docs describe agent/chat/edit surfaces rather than autocomplete.

citations.label: Use Claude Code with Pro or Max plan

recommendation.text: Use them alongside a separate autocomplete tool if you already have subscriptions.

limitations: Not an Obsidian true inline solution.

rejected_or_weak_options.name: Unofficial ChatGPT/Claude browser-session or cookie proxy
rejected_or_weak_options.reason: Unsupported access boundary, likely policy/security/account risk, brittle, and poor latency fit for keystroke-level autocomplete.

rejected_or_weak_options.name: Official OpenAI Codex or Claude Code as the autocomplete engine
rejected_or_weak_options.reason: Subscription-supported official products exist, but checked docs do not show live inline Tab autocomplete.

rejected_or_weak_options.name: Codex Autocomplete as default answer
rejected_or_weak_options.reason: It is the closest found ChatGPT/Codex-subscription autocomplete candidate, but non-official, macOS-only, tiny, and not policy/runtime reviewed.

rejected_or_weak_options.name: twinny as current VS Code default
rejected_or_weak_options.reason: Historically relevant open-source FIM/API extension but archived; use only as reference or if you accept abandonware risk.

rejected_or_weak_options.name: Sourcegraph Cody for individual replacement
rejected_or_weak_options.reason: True autocomplete exists, but sources indicate enterprise-only/no non-enterprise availability and high enterprise pricing.

rejected_or_weak_options.name: Obsidian Smart Composer / Claudian / Claude Panel / Blackglass as live autocomplete
rejected_or_weak_options.reason: Useful chat/agent/edit/context tools and some can use subscriptions/CLIs, but not continuous ghost-text Tab autocomplete.

rejected_or_weak_options.name: GitHub Copilot for Obsidian as replacement
rejected_or_weak_options.reason: It can provide the target UX in Obsidian, but it depends on GitHub Copilot, which the user is trying to replace.

uncertainties_and_followups.uncertainty: No runtime benchmarks were run for latency, suggestion quality, auth flow, keybinding conflicts, or data handling.
uncertainties_and_followups.impact: High for final tool choice, especially Obsidian plugins and Codex Autocomplete.
uncertainties_and_followups.recommended_check: Smoke-test top candidates in disposable VS Code and Obsidian profiles with a small code repo and test vault.

uncertainties_and_followups.uncertainty: Some OpenAI Help Center pages were blocked by Cloudflare/browser challenge in worker environments.
uncertainties_and_followups.impact: Low-to-medium because accessible OpenAI Codex auth/pricing/API docs support the main distinction, but exact Help Center wording remains unquoted.
uncertainties_and_followups.recommended_check: Browser-capable verifier should retrieve OpenAI Help Center pages on ChatGPT vs Platform billing and Codex plan usage.

uncertainties_and_followups.uncertainty: Kilo Code autocomplete billing and data handling were not fully audited.
uncertainties_and_followups.impact: Medium because Kilo is a strong VS Code candidate but its ChatGPT OAuth docs should not be generalized to autocomplete without explicit evidence.
uncertainties_and_followups.recommended_check: Read Kilo Gateway/BYOK pricing/privacy terms and run a local auth/autocomplete test.

citations.label: Kilo Code ChatGPT Plus/Pro provider docs

uncertainties_and_followups.uncertainty: The future Claude Agent SDK credit begins after the report date and may alter economics for third-party agent apps, but not necessarily inline autocomplete.
uncertainties_and_followups.impact: Medium for Claude subscription users after 2026-06-15; low for current autocomplete recommendation.
uncertainties_and_followups.recommended_check: Revisit after launch and verify whether any third-party inline autocomplete plugin uses the Agent SDK credit path.

citations.label: Claude Agent SDK monthly plan credit

uncertainties_and_followups.uncertainty: Pricing, marketplace install counts, and product availability drift quickly.
uncertainties_and_followups.recommended_check: Refresh source URLs before making a paid or organization-wide decision.

source_backed_claims.text: No checked official OpenAI or Anthropic path provides existing-subscription live Tab autocomplete in both VS Code and Obsidian.

citations.label: Claude subscription vs API/Console Help Center article

source_backed_claims.text: Official API use for OpenAI/Anthropic-style arbitrary integrations is separate from normal ChatGPT/Claude UI subscriptions.

citations.label: Claude subscription vs API/Console Help Center article

source_backed_claims.text: True live inline Tab autocomplete exists in Obsidian community plugins, though mostly via small/aging API/local plugins.
A shared backend can serve both VS Code and Obsidian, but each editor still needs native UX integration for context capture, ghost text, cancellation, and Tab acceptance.

source_list.title: ChatGPT Plans | Free, Go, Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise
source_list.quoted_evidence: Plus lists 'Expanded Codex usage'; Pro lists '5x or 20x more usage' and 'more Codex usage'; the page separates ChatGPT plans from API Platform links.

source_list.title: OpenAI API Pricing
source_list.quoted_evidence: The page is titled 'OpenAI API Pricing' and lists token prices such as GPT-5.5 input/cached-input/output per 1M tokens.

source_list.title: API Platform Developer quickstart
source_list.quoted_evidence: The quickstart says 'Make your first API request' and shows 'Create API key' plus an Authorization header using 'Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY'.

source_list.title: IDE extension – Codex | OpenAI Developers
source_list.quoted_evidence: The page says the Codex VS Code extension lets you use Codex side by side in your IDE; 'ChatGPT Plus, Pro, Business, Edu, and Enterprise plans include Codex'; sign-in can use a ChatGPT account or API key.

source_list.title: Features – Codex IDE | OpenAI Developers
source_list.quoted_evidence: The page describes using Codex in the editor to 'chat, edit, and preview changes', choose Chat/Agent/Agent Full Access modes, and delegate work to cloud tasks.

source_list.title: Pricing – Codex | OpenAI Developers
source_list.quoted_evidence: The Codex pricing page has an 'API Key' option: 'Codex in the CLI, SDK, or IDE extension' and 'Pay only for the tokens Codex uses, based on API pricing'; it also says all users may run extra local tasks with an API key charged at standard API rates.

source_list.title: Codex – OpenAI’s coding agent - Visual Studio Marketplace
source_list.quoted_evidence: The listing says Codex is included in ChatGPT Plus, Pro, Business, Edu, and Enterprise plans; it describes adding Codex as a panel in VS Code to chat, edit, preview changes, and delegate tasks to the cloud.

source_list.title: OpenAI Terms of Use
source_list.quoted_evidence: OpenAI says users may not share account credentials, automatically or programmatically extract data or Output, or interfere with services including circumventing rate limits or restrictions.

source_list.title: Plans & Pricing | Claude by Anthropic
source_list.quoted_evidence: The pricing page lists Pro as including Claude Code, Max as everything in Pro with higher usage, Team seats as including Claude Code and Claude Cowork, Enterprise as seat price plus usage at API rates, and an API section with per-MTok token prices.

source_list.title: I have a paid Claude subscription... Why do I have to pay separately to use the Claude API and Console?
source_list.quoted_evidence: The help article says Claude paid plans and Claude Console are separate products; a paid Claude subscription 'doesn't include access to the Claude API or Console' and API access requires separately setting up Console access.

source_list.title: Use Claude Code with your Pro or Max plan
source_list.quoted_evidence: The article says Pro and Max users have access to Claude on web/desktop/mobile and Claude Code in terminal with one unified subscription; it warns that ANTHROPIC_API_KEY makes Claude Code use API charges instead of subscription usage.

source_list.title: Use Claude Code with your Team or Enterprise plan
source_list.quoted_evidence: The article says Claude Code is included with every Team plan seat and included with the single Enterprise seat on new/self-serve plans, with caveats for older Enterprise seat types.

source_list.title: Overview - Claude Code Docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: The overview says Claude Code is an AI-powered coding assistant; most surfaces require a Claude subscription or Anthropic Console account; the VS Code extension provides inline diffs, @-mentions, plan review, and conversation history.

source_list.title: Use Claude Code in VS Code - Claude Code Docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: The VS Code docs call the extension a native graphical interface; it supports reviewing/editing plans, auto-accepting edits, @-mentioning files, conversation history, and multiple conversations.

source_list.title: Claude Code for VS Code - Visual Studio Marketplace
source_list.quoted_evidence: The listing says the extension can use latest Claude models with a Pro, Max, Team, or Enterprise subscription, or pay-as-you-go pricing; it reads/writes code, runs terminal commands with permission, and proposes changes in the editor.

source_list.title: API overview - Claude API Docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: The API overview says the Claude API is at https://api.anthropic.com and requires a Claude Console account plus an API key or Workload Identity Federation.

source_list.title: Rate limits - Claude API Docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: The page describes API spend limits, rate limits, usage tiers, credit purchases, and monthly spend limits for API organizations.

source_list.title: Use the Claude Agent SDK with your Claude plan
source_list.quoted_evidence: The article says Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise users are eligible for a monthly Agent SDK credit starting June 15, 2026, covering Agent SDK usage, claude -p, GitHub Actions, and third-party apps that authenticate with the Claude subscription through the Agent SDK.

source_list.title: Anthropic Consumer Terms of Service
source_list.quoted_evidence: Anthropic says users may not share account login/API key credentials, crawl/scrape/harvest data, access services through automated or non-human means except via an API key or explicit permission, or bypass protective measures.

source_list.title: Anthropic Commercial Terms of Service
source_list.quoted_evidence: The Commercial Terms govern API keys and Console-related services and prohibit reverse engineering or duplicating the services and supporting third-party attempts to do so.

source_list.title: Continue - open-source AI code agent - Visual Studio Marketplace
source_list.quoted_evidence: Marketplace title identifies Continue as an open-source AI code agent for Visual Studio Code.

source_list.title: GitHub API repository metadata for continuedev/continue
source_list.quoted_evidence: Repository metadata: stars 33442, forks 4573, pushed_at 2026-05-28T20:33:23Z, license Apache-2.0, archived false.

source_list.title: Continue Autocomplete Setup and Configuration Guide
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs recommend Codestral through the Mistral API for the best autocomplete experience, recommend Ollama for a local model path, warn thinking-type models are slower, document multilineCompletions, VS Code inlineSuggest settings, and Tab keybinding for accepting lines.

source_list.title: Autocomplete Role in Continue Models
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say an autocomplete model is trained on fill-in-the-middle, used to display inline autocomplete suggestions as you type, and that chat models often perform poorly for this task even with prompting.

source_list.title: Model Providers Overview
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs list Anthropic and OpenAI capabilities as Chat/Edit/Apply/Embeddings, list Ollama with Autocomplete capability, include local options such as LM Studio and llama.cpp, and state most cloud providers require API keys.

source_list.title: Tabby - Visual Studio Marketplace
source_list.quoted_evidence: Marketplace says Tabby is an open-source, self-hosted AI coding assistant and suggests multi-line code completions and full functions in real time as you write code; installs shown as 78,071.

source_list.title: GitHub API repository metadata for TabbyML/tabby
source_list.quoted_evidence: Repository metadata: stars 33550, forks 1747, pushed_at 2026-03-02T20:08:28Z, archived false, description self-hosted AI coding assistant.

source_list.title: Tabby LICENSE raw file
source_list.quoted_evidence: License file says content outside ee/ and other restrictions is available under Apache 2.0; ee/ has its own license if present.

source_list.title: Model Configuration | Tabby
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs define Completion, Chat, and Embedding models; the Completion model focuses on Fill-in-the-Middle prompting; local connections use llama.cpp; remote connectors can connect to APIs of providers like Claude, OpenAI, Ollama, or custom endpoints.

source_list.title: Code Completion | Tabby
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say code completion is a key feature in IDE/extensions, can analyze code repositories or documentation supplied by users, and completion tuning varies by model serving provider such as llama.cpp, vLLM, and TensorRT-LLM.

source_list.title: twinny - AI Code Completion and Chat - Visual Studio Marketplace
source_list.quoted_evidence: Marketplace lists supported providers including localhost, OpenAI/Ollama-compatible API, OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, DeepSeek, Cohere, Mistral AI, Perplexity, and Groq; main features include Fill in the Middle Code Completion and single/multiline FIM completions; states MIT licensed.

source_list.quoted_evidence: Repository metadata: stars 3628, forks 224, pushed_at 2025-08-07T10:12:07Z, license MIT, archived true, description calls it a locally or API-hosted VS Code completion plugin like GitHub Copilot.

source_list.title: Tabnine for Enterprise - Visual Studio Marketplace
source_list.quoted_evidence: Marketplace says Tabnine for Enterprise is exclusively for Tabnine Enterprise customers and connects the Tabnine extension to a dedicated Tabnine server.

source_list.title: Code Completions | Tabnine Docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say code completions are available in VS Code, JetBrains, Visual Studio 2022, and Eclipse; suggestions appear inline in gray; hit Tab to accept; types include whole-line, full-function, and comment-to-code completions.

source_list.title: Privacy | Tabnine Docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say Tabnine never retains or shares code with third parties when using Tabnine models, has a no-train-no-retain policy, sends context windows for inference, and discards code after output is generated.

source_list.title: AI Models | Tabnine Docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say Tabnine's AI coding assistance is backed by proprietary Tabnine models for code completions and chat, while Tabnine Chat can optionally use third-party models such as Claude and GPT models.

source_list.title: Deployment Options | Tabnine Docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs list Secure SaaS and Enterprise private installation options including VPC, on-premises, and completely air-gapped environments.

source_list.title: Windsurf Plugin (formerly Codeium) - Visual Studio Marketplace
source_list.quoted_evidence: Marketplace says the Windsurf VSCode Plugin provides autocomplete, chat, and search in 70+ languages with lightning fast speeds, unlimited single and multi-line code completions forever, and free installation; installs shown as 3,779,431.

source_list.title: Autocomplete Overview | Windsurf Docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say Windsurf Autocomplete is powered by in-house models optimized for speed and accuracy, makes in-line and multi-line suggestions based on code context, shows gray text as you type, and accepts suggestions with Tab.

source_list.title: Autocomplete Tips | Windsurf Docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say Windsurf Autocomplete can use inline comments and can Fill In The Middle (FIM).

source_list.title: Windsurf Tab | Windsurf Docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say Windsurf Tab is powered by a custom in-house model, uses code, terminal, Cascade chat history, prior editor actions, and optional clipboard context, accepts suggestions with Tab, and offers Autocomplete and Supercomplete modes.

source_list.title: Windsurf Editor | Windsurf
source_list.quoted_evidence: Product page says the full power of Windsurf Tab is exclusive to the Windsurf Editor and IDE plugins include only the autocomplete action.

source_list.title: Context Awareness Overview | Windsurf Docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say Windsurf's RAG context engine indexes the entire local codebase, uses current/open files and relevant snippets, offers expanded context to Pro users, and remote repository indexing for Teams and Enterprise.

source_list.title: Plans and Pricing | Windsurf
source_list.quoted_evidence: Pricing page lists Free $0/month, Pro $20/month, Max $200/month, Teams $40/user/month, and Enterprise contact sales; page lists Tab, Fast Context, SWE model, and premium models as plan features.

source_list.title: Supermaven - Visual Studio Marketplace
source_list.quoted_evidence: Marketplace says Supermaven uses a 1 million token context window for code completion and low latency, offers fast high-quality suggestions, supports large codebases and 70+ languages, and chat can use OpenAI/Anthropic API keys; installs shown as 385,099.

source_list.title: Supermaven: Free AI Code Completion
source_list.quoted_evidence: Homepage claims 250ms latency versus 783ms competitor, a 1 million token context window, VS Code compatibility, free tier, Pro $10/month, and a 7-day data retention limit.

source_list.title: Supermaven joins Cursor
source_list.quoted_evidence: Cursor blog dated Nov 12, 2024 says Supermaven is joining Cursor; the Supermaven plugin will remain maintained, though Cursor will be the core focus.

source_list.title: Cody: AI Code Assistant - Visual Studio Marketplace
source_list.quoted_evidence: Marketplace says Cody brings autocomplete, chat, and commands, but prominently states Cody is no longer available for non-enterprise users; installs shown as 844,233.

source_list.title: Cody - Sourcegraph docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say Cody is supported on Sourcegraph Enterprise, available on VS Code/JetBrains/Visual Studio/Web, uses latest LLMs and development context, pulls context from local and remote codebases, and has chat, completions, code edits, and prompts.

source_list.title: Autocomplete - Sourcegraph docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say Cody offers single-line and multi-line suggestions; autocomplete is optimized for server/client performance; enterprise users default to DeepSeek V2; setup requires Sourcegraph Enterprise with Cody enabled; suggestions appear as grayed text and Enter or Tab accepts.

source_list.title: Pricing | Sourcegraph
source_list.quoted_evidence: Pricing page lists an Enterprise plan starting at $16K, credits for AI features, single-tenant cloud, self-hosted, and enterprise-grade security/admin controls.

source_list.title: Cursor: The best coding agent
source_list.quoted_evidence: Homepage says Cursor has a specialized Tab model that predicts the next action with speed and precision, and lets users choose models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, xAI, and Cursor.

source_list.title: Tab completion | Cursor Docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say Tab is Cursor's AI-powered autocomplete, suggests code as you type based on recent edits, surrounding code, and linter errors, appears as grayed text, accepts full suggestion with Tab, can modify multiple lines and suggest cross-file edits.

source_list.title: Cursor Pricing
source_list.quoted_evidence: Pricing lists Hobby Free with limited Tab completions, Individual Pro $20/month with frontier models, Teams $40/user/month with team-wide privacy mode, and Enterprise custom.

source_list.title: How can I access the Claude API? | Claude Help Center
source_list.quoted_evidence: Help article says organizations can create a Claude Console account; Console is where users create API keys, add users, set up billing, and experiment with Claude on Workbench.

source_list.title: I have a paid Claude subscription... Why do I have to pay separately to use the Claude API and Console?
source_list.quoted_evidence: Help article says Claude paid plans and Claude Console are separate products; a paid Claude subscription enhances chat but does not include access to the Claude API or Console.

source_list.title: How do I pay for my Claude API usage? | Claude Help Center
source_list.quoted_evidence: Help article says Claude API and Workbench usage is billed via prepaid usage credits purchased before using the API.

source_list.title: Obsidian community plugin registry
source_list.quoted_evidence: Fetched official community-plugins.json; entries include plugin id, name, author, description, and repo for 4234 community plugins.

source_list.title: Obsidian community plugin stats
source_list.quoted_evidence: Fetched official community-plugin-stats.json; entries include downloads and updated timestamps by plugin id.

source_list.title: Editor extensions - Obsidian Developer Documentation
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say Obsidian uses CodeMirror 6 to power the Markdown editor, and that an Obsidian editor extension is the same thing as a CodeMirror extension; examples use registerEditorExtension in plugin onload.

source_list.title: Plugin.registerEditorExtension() - Obsidian Developer Documentation
source_list.quoted_evidence: API docs: registerEditorExtension registers a CodeMirror 6 extension; the extension parameter must be a CodeMirror 6 Extension or array of Extensions.

source_list.title: Decorations - Obsidian Developer Documentation
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs: decorations let plugins change the editor appearance; widget decorations insert elements in the document.

source_list.title: EditorSuggest - Obsidian Developer Documentation
source_list.quoted_evidence: API docs define EditorSuggest as extending PopoverSuggest and include getSuggestions, onTrigger, renderSuggestion, and selectSuggestion methods.

source_list.quoted_evidence: README/official listing: completes words like IDE auto-completion. It is not an AI/GPT plugin. Repo status snapshot: pushed_at=2026-05-10T05:44:37Z; updated_at=2026-05-24T19:23:16Z; stars=898; open_issues=60; archived=False.

source_list.title: Text Autocomplete - Obsidian Plugin
source_list.quoted_evidence: Official listing/registry: Autocomplete text to type more efficiently.; downloads=1499; registry_updated=2026-03-14; repo=c-degni/text-autocomplete.

source_list.title: GitHub repository: c-degni/text-autocomplete
source_list.quoted_evidence: README: utilize text autocomplete; selecting a highlighted suggestion with enter/tab; official listing says autocomplete text to type more efficiently. Repo status snapshot: pushed_at=2026-03-14T23:45:12Z; updated_at=2026-05-17T12:24:02Z; stars=6; open_issues=0; archived=False.

source_list.title: Completr - Obsidian Plugin
source_list.quoted_evidence: Official listing/registry: Advanced auto-completion for LaTeX, frontmatter, and standard writing.; downloads=82038; registry_updated=2023-05-30; repo=tth05/obsidian-completr.

source_list.title: GitHub repository: tth05/obsidian-completr
source_list.quoted_evidence: README: advanced auto-completion for Obsidian; LaTeX/frontmatter/standard writing; legacy editor not supported; not an AI/GPT provider. Repo status snapshot: pushed_at=2024-06-03T01:11:13Z; updated_at=2026-05-13T08:27:52Z; stars=388; open_issues=42; archived=False.

source_list.title: Continue Autocomplete Setup and Configuration Guide
source_list.quoted_evidence: Continue documents Ollama local autocomplete setup and says after running qwen2.5-coder and adding it to config, 'you should begin to see completions in VS Code'; it also cautions that thinking models are slower and less suitable for speed-sensitive autocomplete.

source_list.title: Twinny README
source_list.quoted_evidence: Twinny lists supported providers including 'localhost OpenAI/Ollama Compatible API', OpenAI, Anthropic, and OpenRouter, and lists 'Fill in the Middle Code Completion' with 'AI-powered real-time code suggestions'.

source_list.title: Tabby README
source_list.quoted_evidence: Tabby describes itself as 'a self-hosted AI coding assistant' and an 'open-source and on-premises alternative to GitHub Copilot', with 'OpenAPI interface' and support for consumer-grade GPUs.

source_list.title: Tabby Visual Studio Code Extension Docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: The Tabby VS Code page says Tabby is 'open-source' and 'self-hosted' and that 'Tabby suggests multi-line code completions and full functions in real-time as you write code'.

source_list.title: Tabby Model Configuration
source_list.quoted_evidence: Tabby says its Completion model is for code-completion suggestions and focuses mainly on the Fill-in-the-Middle (FIM) prompting style; its Chat model is broadly compatible with OpenAI standards.

source_list.title: Ollama OpenAI compatibility
source_list.quoted_evidence: Ollama says it provides compatibility with parts of the OpenAI API and documents /v1/chat/completions with supported features including Chat completions and Streaming.

source_list.title: Ollama Generate a response API
source_list.quoted_evidence: Ollama documents POST /api/generate as generating a response for a provided prompt and shows both default streaming NDJSON behavior and non-streaming with 'stream': false.

source_list.title: llama.cpp HTTP Server README
source_list.quoted_evidence: The server README lists OpenAI-compatible chat completions, responses, and embeddings, and documents POST /infill 'For code infilling' which 'takes a prefix and a suffix and returns the predicted completion as stream'.

source_list.title: vLLM Online Serving
source_list.quoted_evidence: vLLM documents an OpenAI-compatible server supporting /v1/completions, /v1/responses, and /v1/chat/completions; for /v1/completions it notes the 'suffix parameter is not supported'.

source_list.title: LiteLLM Getting Started
source_list.quoted_evidence: LiteLLM describes itself as an open-source library with a unified interface to call 100+ LLMs using the OpenAI format, plus a self-hosted LLM gateway/proxy with virtual keys, cost tracking, and admin UI.

source_list.title: OpenRouter Quickstart
source_list.quoted_evidence: OpenRouter says it provides a unified API to hundreds of AI models via a single endpoint, documents /api/v1/chat/completions, supports streaming, and can use the OpenAI SDK as a drop-in replacement.

source_list.title: Qwen2.5-Coder-7B model card
source_list.quoted_evidence: The model card lists license apache-2.0 and says Qwen2.5-Coder can be used for 'fill in the middle tasks'.

source_list.title: StarCoder2-7B model card
source_list.quoted_evidence: The model card says StarCoder2-7B was trained using the Fill-in-the-Middle objective on 3.5+ trillion tokens and lists license bigcode-openrail-m.

source_list.title: DeepSeek Coder 6.7B base model card
source_list.quoted_evidence: The model card says the models use a 16K window and an extra fill-in-the-blank task to support project-level code completion and infilling, and includes an FIM-token example.

source_list.title: CodeLlama-7B model card
source_list.quoted_evidence: The model card lists model capabilities as code completion and infilling, but notes the repository is a non-official mirror and the license is llama2.

source_list.title: Mistral coding and FIM documentation
source_list.quoted_evidence: Mistral documents Codestral as specifically trained for Code Generation and FIM and documents /v1/fim/completions as a Fill-in-the-middle endpoint for code completion and generation supporting codestral-latest.

source_list.title: How can I move my ChatGPT subscription to the API?
source_list.quoted_evidence: OpenAI Help says the API service is billed and managed separately to ChatGPT, can be upgraded to pay-as-you-go by adding a payment method in API billing settings, and API usage is charged by tokens used.

source_list.title: Paid Claude subscription versus Claude API and Console
source_list.quoted_evidence: Claude Help says Claude paid plans and Claude Console are separate products; a paid Claude subscription enhances chat but doesn't include access to the Claude API or Console, and API usage requires separately setting up Console access.

source_list.title: Claude API access and billing help
source_list.quoted_evidence: Claude Help says the Claude Console is where users create API keys, add users, set up billing, and experiment with Workbench.

source_list.title: Use Claude Code with your Pro or Max plan
source_list.quoted_evidence: Claude Help says Pro and Max subscriptions can access Claude Code in the terminal with one unified subscription; it also notes an ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable makes Claude Code use API charges instead of subscription usage.

source_list.title: VS Code vscode.d.ts InlineCompletionItemProvider
source_list.quoted_evidence: VS Code's API declares InlineCompletionItemProvider for inline completions requested implicitly while typing and declares languages.registerInlineCompletionItemProvider to register such providers.

source_list.title: Obsidian API TypeScript definitions
source_list.quoted_evidence: The Obsidian API exposes registerEditorExtension(extension: Extension) for CodeMirror 6 extensions and defines EditorSuggest<T> extending PopoverSuggest<T> for editor suggestion UIs.

source_list.title: CodeMirror Reference Manual - Decoration and WidgetType
source_list.quoted_evidence: CodeMirror documents Decoration.widget as creating a widget decoration that displays a DOM element at a position; positive side draws after the cursor at the same position, and inline widget is the default.

source_list.title: Authentication – Codex | OpenAI Developers
source_list.quoted_evidence: Codex supports 'Sign in with ChatGPT for subscription access' and 'Sign in with an API key for usage-based access'; API key usage is billed through the OpenAI Platform at standard API rates.

source_list.title: Pricing – Codex | OpenAI Developers
source_list.quoted_evidence: Plus includes Codex on the web, CLI, IDE extension, and iOS; API Key use is 'Pay only for the tokens Codex uses, based on API pricing'; Pro includes access to GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark research preview, which is not available in the API at launch.

source_list.title: IDE extension – Codex | OpenAI Developers
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say the Codex VS Code extension lets users use Codex side by side or delegate tasks to Codex Cloud; after install it appears in the editor sidebar; setup prompts sign-in with ChatGPT account or API key.

source_list.title: Features – Codex IDE | OpenAI Developers
source_list.quoted_evidence: Feature docs describe prompting Codex to chat, edit, preview changes, switch models, choose approval modes, delegate to cloud, use web search, and generate images; no inline Tab autocomplete feature was found in this page.

source_list.title: Codex Autocomplete - Visual Studio Marketplace
source_list.quoted_evidence: Listing says this is a free, open-source VS Code extension with Cursor-style inline ghost text completions using Codex OAuth, Tab to accept, and existing Codex plan limits or credits; it warns 'This is not an official OpenAI extension' and currently only macOS is supported. Marketplace API reported 408 installs, 131 downloads, lastUpdated 2026-03-20, version 1.0.1.

source_list.title: GitHub repository: kevchan9922/codex_autocomplete
source_list.quoted_evidence: README repeats Codex OAuth, inline ghost text, Tab accept, full-line and partial-line completion, and non-official warning. GitHub API: created 2026-03-09, pushed 2026-03-23, 3 stars, 2 forks, archived false, MIT license.

source_list.title: codex_autocomplete configuration.md
source_list.quoted_evidence: Configuration docs set default endpoint to https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses for OAuth flow and https://api.openai.com/v1/responses for API key flow; settings include automatic|hotkey trigger, latency, context, model, and rate limits.

source_list.title: CodexComplete - Visual Studio Marketplace
source_list.quoted_evidence: Listing says CodexComplete provides Copilot-style inline suggestions using OpenAI API models, requires command 'Set OpenAI API Key', stores the API key in SecretStorage, and has local diagnostics; Marketplace API reported 49 installs, 202 downloads, lastUpdated 2026-04-27, version 0.0.16.

source_list.title: Autocomplete | Kilo Code docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say Kilo Code autocomplete uses Fill-in-the-Middle routed through the Kilo Gateway, is enabled by default, shows inline ghost text accepted with Tab, and supports Codestral billed through Kilo or free with BYOK Mistral plus Mercury Edit 2 via BYOK.

source_list.title: Kilo Code: AI Coding Agent, Copilot, and Autocomplete - Visual Studio Marketplace
source_list.quoted_evidence: Listing calls Kilo an open-source AI coding agent with 'Inline Autocomplete' and 'API keys optional'. Marketplace API reported 1,142,537 installs, rating 3.81 over 187 ratings, lastUpdated 2026-05-28, version 7.3.16.

source_list.title: Using ChatGPT Plus/Pro with Kilo Code
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say ChatGPT Plus or Pro can run OpenAI coding models inside Kilo Code with OAuth and no API key; usage counts against ChatGPT subscription rather than separate API usage; docs list full agentic workflows and core functionality, while cloud features require Kilo Gateway.

source_list.title: GitHub API repository metadata for Kilo-Org/kilocode
source_list.quoted_evidence: Repository metadata: unarchived, pushed_at 2026-05-28T21:19:13Z, stars 19665, forks 2605, open issues 1257, license MIT.

source_list.title: ChatGPT Plus/Pro | Roo Code Documentation
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs say Roo Code can select API Provider 'OpenAI – ChatGPT Plus/Pro', sign in to OpenAI Codex, and count usage against ChatGPT subscription rather than separate API usage; arbitrary OpenAI API models are not available.

source_list.title: GitHub API repository metadata for RooCodeInc/Roo-Code
source_list.quoted_evidence: Repository metadata: archived true, pushed_at 2026-05-15T18:08:47Z, stars 24166, forks 3281, license Apache-2.0.

source_list.title: Use Claude Code in VS Code - Claude Code Docs
source_list.quoted_evidence: Docs describe Claude Code as a VS Code graphical interface for plans, diffs, @-mentions, prompt box, permissions, sessions, and terminal access; the page does not document inline ghost-text/tab autocomplete.

source_list.title: Feature request: Inline/tab code completions in VS Code and JetBrains extensions
source_list.quoted_evidence: Issue text states Claude Code's IDE extensions do not support inline tab completions and asks for completions using existing Claude Code subscription. This is user-reported issue evidence, not official product documentation.

source_list.title: Use the Claude Agent SDK with your Claude plan
source_list.quoted_evidence: Starting June 15, 2026, eligible Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise users can claim a monthly Agent SDK credit for Agent SDK usage, claude -p, and third-party apps built on Agent SDK; over-credit usage moves to usage credits at standard API rates if enabled.

source_list.title: GitHub repository: YishenTu/claudian
source_list.quoted_evidence: README says Claudian embeds AI coding agents Claude Code, Codex, and Opencode in Obsidian, with chat sidebar, inline edit, slash commands, plan mode, file read/write/search/bash, Claude subscription/API requirement, and optional Codex CLI. GitHub API: 11976 stars, pushed 2026-05-25, archived false, MIT.

source_list.title: Obsidian community plugin registry entry for Claudian
source_list.quoted_evidence: Registry entry id=realclaudian, name=Claudian, repo=yishentu/claudian, description says it embeds Claude Code/Codex as an AI collaborator in the vault with file read/write, search, bash commands, and multi-step workflows; stats JSON reported downloads=729914, updated=2026-05-23T12:23:36Z.

source_list.title: GitHub repository: Roasbeef/obsidian-claude-code
source_list.quoted_evidence: README says it embeds Claude as a persistent Obsidian sidebar assistant using the Claude Agent SDK, requiring Anthropic API key or Claude Max/Pro subscription via CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN; it exposes Claude Code tools and Obsidian-specific tools. GitHub API: 206 stars, pushed 2026-01-28, archived false.
